mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean performance in periods of falling rates, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in the industrial production index reflects capacity constraints and fading marginal productivity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production deceleration coincides with positive 20-day equity returns during late-cycle phases, or the predictive power vanishes when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL values correlate with positive 5-day forward returns, or the monthly inflation change shows no statistically significant negative relationship with short-term equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent, robust aggregate demand supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to precede positive 60-day equity returns, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior earnings momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative or flat 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rising federal funds rate indicates policy over-tightening that suppresses risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during recovery phases coincide with positive 60-day equity returns, or the regime-specific predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.
